examples.title: Algorithm: Master-Slave I/O Pattern with Scatter/Gather
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating serial I/O where a master process handles all file operations and distributes data to worker processes using scatter/gather operations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Parallel_IO_With_Scatter_Gather
INPUT: Input filename, output filename, number of processes (n)
OUTPUT: Processed results written to output file

BEGIN
 // Determine process rank and total processes
 rank ← Get_Rank()
 n ← Get_Size()
 
 // === INPUT PHASE ===
 IF rank = 0 THEN
 // Master process reads entire input file
 PRINT "Master process reading input file..."
 full_data ← READ_FILE(input_filename)
 total_elements ← LENGTH(full_data)
 END IF
 
 // Broadcast total number of elements to all processes
 IF rank = 0 THEN
 total_elements ← BROADCAST(total_elements, root=0)
 ELSE
 total_elements ← BROADCAST(nil, root=0)
 END IF
 
 // Calculate elements per process (block decomposition)
 elements_per_proc ← total_elements / n
 remainder ← total_elements % n
 
 // Distribute counts and displacements to all processes
 sendcounts ← CALCULATE_SCATTER_COUNTS(n, elements_per_proc, remainder)
 displacements ← CALCULATE_SCATTER_DISPLACEMENTS(n, elements_per_proc, remainder)
 
 // Allocate local buffer for each process
 local_data ← ALLOCATE(sendcounts[rank])
 
 // Scatter data from master to all processes
 IF rank = 0 THEN
 local_data ← full_data[0 : sendcounts[0]]
 END IF
 SCATTERV(full_data, sendcounts, displacements, local_data, root=0)
 
 // === COMPUTATION PHASE ===
 PRINT "Process ", rank, " processing ", LENGTH(local_data), " elements"
 local_result ← PROCESS(local_data)
 
 // === OUTPUT PHASE ===
 // Gather all local results back to master
 IF rank = 0 THEN
 full_result ← ALLOCATE(total_elements)
 ELSE
 full_result ← nil
 END IF
 
 GATHERV(local_result, sendcounts, displacements, full_result, root=0)
 
 // Master writes final output
 IF rank = 0 THEN
 PRINT "Master process writing output file..."
 WRITE_FILE(output_filename, full_result)
 END IF
 
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the master-slave I/O pattern. The master process (rank 0) reads all input data from disk, then uses MPI_Scatterv to distribute chunks to all processes. Each process computes on its local chunk, then MPI_Gatherv collects results back to the master which writes the final output. This approach avoids simultaneous file access from multiple processes but creates a bottleneck at the master process.

examples.title: Python: MPI I/O with mpi4py Scatter/Gather
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating serial I/O pattern using mpi4py library with scatter and gather operations
examples.code:
from mpi4py import MPI
import numpy as np
import sys

def read_input_file(filename):
 """Master process reads all data from input file"""
 data = np.loadtxt(filename, dtype=np.float64)
 return data

def process_data(local_data):
 """Each process performs computation on its local data chunk"""
 # Example: compute sum of squares for each element
 return local_data ** 2

def write_output_file(filename, data):
 """Master process writes results to output file"""
 np.savetxt(filename, data, fmt='%.6f')

def main():
 # Initialize MPI communicator
 comm = MPI.COMM_WORLD
 rank = comm.Get_rank()
 size = comm.Get_size()
 
 if len(sys.argv) != 3:
 if rank == 0:
 print("Usage: mpirun -n <num_procs> python program.py <input.txt> <output.txt>")
 sys.exit(1)
 
 input_file = sys.argv[1]
 output_file = sys.argv[2]
 
 # === INPUT PHASE ===
 if rank == 0:
 print(f"Master (Rank {rank}): Reading input from {input_file}")
 full_data = read_input_file(input_file)
 total_elements = len(full_data)
 else:
 full_data = None
 total_elements = None
 
 # Broadcast total number of elements to all processes
 total_elements = comm.bcast(total_elements, root=0)
 
 # Calculate scatter parameters for balanced distribution
 base_count = total_elements // size
 remainder = total_elements % size
 
 # Each process gets different count based on remainder distribution
 if rank < remainder:
 local_count = base_count + 1
 else:
 local_count = base_count
 
 # Calculate displacement for each process
 displacements = np.zeros(size, dtype=int)
 sendcounts = np.zeros(size, dtype=int)
 
 for i in range(size):
 if i < remainder:
 sendcounts[i] = base_count + 1
 else:
 sendcounts[i] = base_count
 displacements[i] = sum(sendcounts[:i])
 
 # Allocate local buffer
 local_data = np.zeros(local_count, dtype=np.float64)
 
 # Scatter data to all processes
 comm.Scatterv([full_data, sendcounts, displacements, MPI.DOUBLE], 
 [local_data, local_count, MPI.DOUBLE], root=0)
 
 # === COMPUTATION PHASE ===
 print(f"Rank {rank}: Processing {local_count} elements")
 local_result = process_data(local_data)
 
 # === OUTPUT PHASE ===
 # Gather results back to master
 full_result = np.zeros(total_elements, dtype=np.float64) if rank == 0 else None
 
 comm.Gatherv([local_result, local_count, MPI.DOUBLE],
 [full_result, sendcounts, displacements, MPI.DOUBLE], 
 root=0)
 
 # Master writes output
 if rank == 0:
 print(f"Master: Writing output to {output_file}")
 write_output_file(output_file, full_result)
 print("Processing complete!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
 main()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses mpi4py to demonstrate the master-slave I/O pattern. The master process (rank 0) reads all input data using NumPy, calculates proper scatter counts and displacements for balanced distribution, then uses Scatterv to distribute data chunks to all processes. Each process performs computation (squaring each element) on its local chunk, then Gatherv collects results back to the master which writes the final output. Time complexity is O(n/p) for computation plus O(n) for communication.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for full data on master, O(n/p) per worker process

examples.title: C: MPI-IO Parallel File Access with File Views
examples.description: C implementation demonstrating MPI-IO for parallel file reading using file views and collective operations
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates MPI-IO for parallel file access. Each process opens the same file and uses MPI_File_set_view to define a unique file view (a specific offset and data type) that makes only its portion visible. MPI_File_read_all performs a collective read where all processes cooperate to efficiently read their respective chunks. This approach eliminates the master bottleneck and allows direct parallel access to different parts of the file, leveraging parallel file system capabilities. Time complexity is O(n/p) for I/O and computation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) for parallel I/O and computation
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n/p) per process for local buffers
